label: Retrieval-augmented generation

node.label: Retrieval-augmented generation
node.definition: A generator conditions on retrieved non-parametric evidence so knowledge can be updated and attributed externally.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Retrieval-augmented generation is to connect Dense vector retrieval, Hybrid retrieval & reranking to downstream capabilities including Graph RAG & structured retrieval, Provenance, citations & evidence tracking, Multimodal assistants & vision-language systems.
examples: substrate for Graph RAG & structured retrieval
substrate for Provenance, citations & evidence tracking
substrate for Multimodal assistants & vision-language systems
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include Dense vector retrieval, Hybrid retrieval & reranking.

dependencies.rationale: Retrieval-augmented generation stores, selects, or obtains information through Dense vector retrieval.
Retrieval-augmented generation stores, selects, or obtains information through Hybrid retrieval & reranking.
Retrieval-augmented generation uses Decoder-only causal transformers as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Retrieval-augmented generation uses External retrieval & ranking as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
